_comment_yield_average_horizon_turns: The forward-projection horizon (in turns) for a source's steady realized yield. SourceYield.realized is the AVERAGE food/turn the source delivers over the next N turns, computed by simulating the herd/patch forward N turns from its current state under the assignment's policy + worker count (the smooth policy rate, no kill-credit bank). A pure function of state, so the assign-time seed and the resolved row are identical (exact forecast==actual). Larger = a settled Sustain herd reads flat ≈ MSY, and a Surplus/Market projection sees the herd's decline within the window. Its OWN lever, NOT the expedition forecast_horizon_turns (a raid-length horizon). PLAYTEST DIAL. Validated > 0.
_comment_arrivals_horizon_turns: The forward-projection horizon (in turns) for a source's ARRIVAL SCHEDULE. SourceYield.arrivals[i] is the food landing i+1 turns from now, from the SAME forward simulation the realized average runs but WITH the kill-credit bank — so it answers 'on which turns does the food actually arrive', not 'how much per turn on average'. A big-game Sustain hunt is genuinely lumpy (zeros between hauls); a forage patch or fast game lands something every turn. Kept SEPARATE from yield_average_horizon_turns (40) deliberately: this is a display span (how far ahead the larder chart plans), that one is a smoothing window. PLAYTEST DIAL. Validated > 0.

forage._comment_capacity_by_biome: THE HUMAN FOOD WEB, BY BIOME — the exact mirror of fauna_config.json's graze.capacity_by_biome (the ANIMAL food web), and the other half of the two-stock split (docs/plan_grazing_foundation.md §1). Human-edible biomass (seeds, nuts, tubers, fruit, shellfish, inshore fish) a tile of each biome carries at capacity; every FoodModuleTag tile is seeded a patch at its biome's value, and a biome reading 0 carries NO PATCH AT ALL. A DATA TABLE OVER THE 38 BIOMES, NOT A FORMULA: every biome must appear (validate() rejects a partial table — a missing row would silently read as an invisible zero-forage dead zone; ZERO MUST BE STATED). Per-BIOME, not per-FoodModule, deliberately: the two tables must be directly comparable tile-for-tile and must be able to DISAGREE WITHIN a module — that disagreement is the agropastoral decision. The FoodModuleTag model is untouched; the module still says what KIND of gathering a tile offers (and its seasonal_weight), this table says HOW MUCH is there. THE HEADLINE INVERSIONS, against the graze table: MixedWoodland 190 forage vs 55 graze (nuts, mast, berries under a canopy that shades out the ground cover — the flagship case); Floodplain/RiverDelta/AlluvialPlain 195-210 forage vs 130-140 graze (silt + water = CROPLAND, the farm, not the pasture); PrairieSteppe 70 forage vs 240 graze (grass: the animals feast, the humans get seed heads). Retired: the old flat carrying_capacity of 120 on every food-module tile, which made one food web spatial and the other a constant and so made 'your best farm is not your best pasture' UNTRUE BY CONSTRUCTION.
forage._comment_cultivation_moved_dials: The plant rung-2 BUILD dials (progress_per_turn, decay_per_turn and the old cultivating_yield_fraction) live in intensification_ladder.json -> the plant:tended rung's `build` block, and as of slice 4 so do the earned-knowledge dials (the old knowledge_progress_per_turn 0.05 / knowledge_completion_threshold 1.0 -> the ladder's `knowledge` block, same values, previously duplicated verbatim in fauna_config's husbandry). Both food webs climb — and now LEARN — on the same numbers. What remains here is the plant web's own economy.
forage._comment_cultivation: THE PLANT LADDER'S PAYOFFS — what each completed rung buys you. The BUILD dials (how long each rung takes, its feral decay, its investment dip) live in intensification_ladder.json; only the payoffs are here, exactly as fauna_config keeps pastoral_gain/pen_gain beside the ladder's build block. THE TWO RUNGS PAY IN DIFFERENT CURRENCIES, AND THAT ASYMMETRY IS THE MODEL (slice 7): RUNG 2 (tended) IS STILL A WILD STAND, better cared for — but since Flora Roster S2 a NEUTRAL one: its patch regrows at the WILD rate (tended_regrowth_gain 1.0), and you gather it under the full Sustain/Surplus/Market/Eradicate axis, drawing it down and able to over-farm it, exactly as a wild patch. A tended patch's payoff over wild comes NOT from a regrowth boost but from a COMMITTED CROP — concentration (redistributing the tile's own K, Flora Roster S1) plus conversion (a good crop's per-species yield.provisions_per_biomass beating the wild base). It began as the plant twin of a tamed PASTORAL herd hunted on its boosted r (husbandry.pastoral_gain 2.0), but the plant rung deliberately DIVERGED from the animal one here: the animal lever stays at 2.0 while tended_regrowth_gain dropped to 1.0, so they are no longer the same value. RUNG 3 (field) IS YOURS — you control its reproduction, so there is no wild stock left to over-skim, the policy axis honestly collapses, and it pays a flat managed rate on its standing crop without drawdown (the pen's twin). RETIRED: tended_provisions_per_biomass 0.01, which made rung 2 a managed rate a full rung EARLIER than the animal side — a tended patch could not be over-farmed and every policy forecast the identical number. S2 RETIRED THE REGROWTH BOOST: tended_regrowth_gain 2.0 -> 1.0 (NEUTRAL — a tended stand regrows exactly as fast as wild). After Flora Roster S1 made CONCENTRATION explicit (redistributing the tile's K, docs/plan_flora_roster.md §4.3), a growth-rate boost double-counts competitor-removal, so tending now pays purely through concentration + CONVERSION (a committed crop's yield.provisions_per_biomass exceeding the wild base). The rung-2 'wild < tended' guarantee therefore MOVED to the roster's own bar (core_sim/tests/flora_roster.rs — worth on best country, not worst), which sees concentration and conversion; this scale-free config check no longer polices it. The lever stays (1.0 = neutral, a playtest dial if a small boost is wanted back). VALIDATED: field_provisions_per_biomass > gain x regrowth_rate/4 x provisions_per_biomass (tended < field, still monotone and scale-free); the tended_regrowth_gain check now only forbids the INCOHERENT case gain < 1.0 (tending making a stand grow SLOWER than wild). Measured on K=195 AlluvialPlain, uncommitted patch production per turn: wild Sustain 0.61 = tended 0.61 -> Field 3.90 (a committed good crop's tended rate rises above wild via conversion). PLAYTEST DIALS.
forage._comment_concentration: THE LAND OWNS K (docs/plan_flora_roster.md §4.3). Committing a patch to one named plant (Cultivate/Sow, flora_config.json) does NOT add capacity — it REDISTRIBUTES the capacity the tile already carries: concentration = min(1.0, share x the rung's gain), and the patch's effective carrying capacity becomes tile_K x concentration. THE CAP AT 1.0 IS THE MODEL, NOT A SAFETY RAIL: rungs 1-3 may only redistribute the K the land already has, because raising K itself is clearing, irrigation and manuring — rung 4 (Worked Land), by definition. The consequence is the whole point: a WILD patch already yields the entire basket (1.0 K), so no concentration gain can ever beat it, and CONCENTRATION ALONE CAN NEVER MAKE TENDING WORTH IT. Tending pays in CONVERSION — the committed species' yield.provisions_per_biomass, which is the roster's lever — or it does not pay. The rule the roster is tuned against is exactly: tending is worth it iff concentration x species_rate > 1.0 x provisions_per_biomass. An UNCOMMITTED patch is untouched by all of this (full tile K, the flat wild rate), so rung 1 is bit-identical to pre-roster. Validated finite and >= 1.0: below 1 a rung would concentrate a plant LESS than its own wild share, which is incoherent. PLAYTEST DIALS.
forage._comment_navigable_river: The capacity_by_biome NavigableRiver row (130) is VESTIGIAL — a navigable hex now reads its UNDERLYING biome (resource_terrain) plus navigable_river_forage_bonus, so the row is bypassed. Left only to keep the table total.
